sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current objects in game area:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 9:  # game area
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Compare with previous frame
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
print("\nPrevious objects in game area:")
for node in prev_seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 9:
        print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see what happened:
- Before SPACE: id=13 was a white (W) block with 100 pixels at [34,27]-[43,36]
- After SPACE: id=13 is now purple (p) with 50 pixels at [34,27]-[38,36], and id=14 is white (W) with 50 pixels at [39,27]-[43,36]
So SPACE caused the white block to split - the top half turned purple and the bottom half stayed white. It's like the agent "painted" or "converted" the top portion of the white block to purple. The white block moved down.
This suggests SPACE projects the agent's color downward onto the target. The goal might be to convert the entire white block to purple. Let me try SPACE again to see if more of the white block converts.
